codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully resolves type inconsistencies between ixland-libc headers and ixland-system implementation. The changes include: (1) Fixed forward declaration mismatches in linux/types.h by changing iox_task_t, iox_files_t, and iox_sighand_t from pointer typedefs to struct typedefs to match task.h definitions; (2) Fixed iox_setgroups signature inconsistency between linux/unistd.h and grp.h; (3) Added IOX_POLLFD_DEFINED and IOX_EPOLL_EVENT_DEFINED guards to prevent duplicate struct definitions; (4) Added IOX_FD_* macro guards in linux/poll.h; (5) Created comprehensive test file test_cross_integration_headers.c that validates header inclusion, type consistency, and forward declaration matching.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The naming convention for type guards (IOX_POLLFD_DEFINED, IOX_EPOLL_EVENT_DEFINED) is consistent with the documented IOX_ prefix for macros. The forward declaration comments added to linux/types.h explicitly document the relationship with task.h, which improves maintainability.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-libc/include/linux/types.h:107-117 - Added detailed comments explaining typedef must match task.h exactly

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test file documents an important architectural decision: iox/iox_syscalls.h uses standard system types and should NOT be mixed with linux/*.h headers which use Linux-specific iox_* types. This design provides clean separation between standard POSIX syscall API and Linux-compatible kernel ABI.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-system/Tests/unit/test_cross_integration_headers.c:17-25 - Comprehensive documentation of header usage patterns

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the standard procedure correctly. The discovered issues about pthread_sigmask type mismatch and POLLIN/POLLOUT conflicts were correctly identified as pre-existing issues not introduced by this feature, and were properly documented in the discoveredIssues array with appropriate severity (non_blocking).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues - Both issues marked as pre-existing with suggested fixes documented

sharedStateObservations.observation: The iox_setgroups signature was correctly aligned between linux/unistd.h and grp.h using size_t for the first parameter and const gid_t* for the second, matching POSIX conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-libc/include/linux/unistd.h:508 - Changed from 'int iox_setgroups(int, const gid_t[])' to 'int iox_setgroups(size_t, const gid_t*)'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test file demonstrates proper usage of atomic types (_Atomic int, _Atomic bool) from task.h, showing the implementation correctly handles thread-safety requirements in the process table.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-system/Tests/unit/test_cross_integration_headers.c:56-67 - Tests atomic operations on task fields

summary: The cross-integration-headers feature has been successfully implemented. All type inconsistencies between ixland-libc headers and ixland-system implementation have been resolved. The forward declarations in linux/types.h now match the struct definitions in task.h. Duplicate struct definitions have been eliminated with proper guards. A comprehensive test file validates VAL-CROSS-001 and VAL-CROSS-002. The build passes successfully with no new warnings or errors. The implementation is production-ready.
